catches a seeded violation of each rule
wired into gate:all
description: New gate tool: checks (1) every docs/*.md has an intro paragraph before the first H2, (2) first acronym uses link to GLOSSARY.md, (3) fenced commands in docs/intro/** are registered in the journey runner, (4) internal links resolve. --json + exit codes; npm script gate:docs; wire into gate:all and CI. Selftest with fixture docs.
title: tools/sma-doc-lint.mjs — keep the explained-ness honest

acceptance_criteria: gate:all exits 0 on pristine clone with actionable WARNs
real violations still exit nonzero (fixture-portfolio negative test)
fresh-clone CI job added
description: Integration finding 2026-07-10: on a fresh public clone with no scans/manifests, gate:rules and gate:scope exit 2 ('no manifests to check') and gate:compliance/gate:license/provenance:verify exit 1 for missing inputs — so gate:all can never pass before first scan. Each gate must distinguish 'nothing to check' (exit 0 + WARN with the doctor-style hint to run npm run scan) from 'checked and failed' (nonzero). Add a fresh-clone CI job asserting gate:all exits 0 on the pristine repo.

evidence.output_tail:
ilds: 0 | blocking: 0 | warnings: 0 | theft-risk: 0

wrote: security/license-gate.generated.json

> smarch@0.1.0 provenance:verify
> node tools/sma-provenance-verify.mjs --coverage --gate

SMA provenance-verify: passed
  seals: 40/40 verified
  signed: no (unsigned hash-chain)
  coverage: 40/0 registry bricks ledgered
  trust: ledger is UNSIGNED — a repo-writer can recompute a consistent head. Sign (sma-provenance-ledger --sign) + anchor externally for tamper-proofing.

> smarch@0.1.0 gate:leaks
> node tools/sma-leak-gate.mjs

SMA leak gate: passed
tracked text files: 681 | hits: 0 | allowed: 0
[rule-gate] WARN — nothing to check; run npm run scan to discover manifests, then rerun this gate
[scope-drift] WARN — nothing to check; run npm run scan to discover manifests, then rerun this gate

title: Gates degrade gracefully on a fresh install (empty registry)

Supports --allow <file> for reviewed exceptions and --json. Add npm script gate:leaks and include it in gate:all.
